steps.title: Find the side length
steps.content: The equilateral triangle has perimeter 852 cm. Since all three sides are equal: $$\text{Side} = \frac{852}{3} = 284 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Apply the circumradius formula for an equilateral triangle
steps.content: When the three vertices lie on a circle, that circle is the circumcircle. For an equilateral triangle with side length $s$, the circumradius is found using the formula. Substituting $s = 284$: $$R = \frac{284\sqrt{3}}{3}$$

steps.title: Solve for w
steps.content: The problem states the radius equals $w\sqrt{3}$. Setting our expression equal to this form: $$\frac{284\sqrt{3}}{3} = w\sqrt{3}$$Dividing both sides by $\sqrt{3}$: $$w = \frac{284}{3}$$
